title: sedum leptophyllum
intro: Sedum leptophyllum, also known as creeping sedum, is a perennial succulent plant native to North America, characterized by its creeping stems and small, white or pink flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Sedum leptophyllum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has also been used in poultices to apply directly to affected areas.
